alignments.pub_claim_text: Tests on a real gene expression dataset unveil interesting new edges that were not revealed by linear SEMs.
alignments.rationale: The real-data finding is preserved with the same level of certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed image representation method accelerates the registration process by effectively triangulating the images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image representation method accelerates the registration process by effectively triangulating the images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about acceleration via triangulation verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall algorithm achieves a high compression rate while retaining the accuracy of the registration compared to the conventional grid-based approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall algorithm achieves a high compression rate while retaining the accuracy of registration compared to the conventional grid-based approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the compression and accuracy trade-off claim with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational time of the registration is significantly reduced using the triangulation-based approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational time of the registration is significantly reduced using the triangulation-based approach.
alignments.rationale: The claim of significant computational time reduction is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepEM method derived from a deep CNN allows automated particle extraction from raw cryo-EM micrographs in the absence of templates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepEM methodology allows automated particle extraction from raw cryo-EM micrographs in the absence of a template.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim about template-free automated particle extraction with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepEM exhibits improved performance and high precision when tested on the standard KLH dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepEM demonstrates improved performance, objectivity, and accuracy compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the evaluation beyond the KLH dataset and adds notions of objectivity/accuracy compared to other methods, changing the claim's emphasis and breadth.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of DeepEM to several challenging experimental cryo-EM datasets demonstrates its capability in avoiding selection of unwanted particles and non-particles.
alignments.rationale: The published version omits the specific result about avoiding unwanted particle selection; no matching publication claim is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of this novel approach is expected to free the labor involved in single-particle verification, thus promoting the efficiency of cryo-EM data processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Application of DeepEM is expected to significantly improve the efficiency of cryo-EM data processing by reducing the labor involved in single-particle verification.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the expectation about reducing verification labor and improving efficiency but phrases it more strongly ("significantly improve") and explicitly links the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A principled way to have data-dependent adaptation of the activation functions is presented, which is performed independently for each neuron.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A principled way to have data-dependent adaptation of the activation functions is presented, which is performed independently for each neuron.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological contribution with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method leverages cubic spline interpolation for local adaptation of the activation functions around their regions of use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method leverages cubic spline interpolation for local adaptation of the activation functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same technical mechanism (cubic spline interpolation for local adaptation); wording is slightly shorter but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting algorithm is relatively cheap to implement and includes a novel damping criterion to counterbalance overfitting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting algorithm is relatively cheap to implement and includes a novel damping criterion to counterbalance overfitting.
alignments.rationale: The implementation cost and damping criterion are retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results validate the proposal over two well-known benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary experimental results validate the proposal.
alignments.rationale: The publication down-weights the empirical claim to "preliminary" results, narrowing and tempering the original stronger benchmark validation statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed globally sparse probabilistic PCA (GSPPCA) allows for the computation of several sparse components with the same sparsity pattern.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian procedure allows for the computation of several sparse components with the same sparsity pattern, facilitating the identification of relevant original variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same technical capability (computing multiple components with shared sparsity); the added facilitation of identification is consistent with the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GSPPCA enables practitioners to identify the original variables that are relevant to describe the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bayesian procedure allows for the computation of several sparse components with the same sparsity pattern, facilitating the identification of relevant original variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly links the procedure to identification of relevant variables, matching the preprint's practical conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first exact computation of the marginal likelihood of a Bayesian PCA model is provided using Roweis' probabilistic interpretation of PCA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The exact computation of the marginal likelihood of a Bayesian PCA model is provided for the first time.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same novelty claim about the first exact marginal-likelihood computation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GSPPCA infers much more relevant gene subsets than traditional sparse PCA algorithms when applied to unlabeled microarray data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GSPPCA vastly surpasses the performance of traditional sparse PCA algorithms in the context of pathway enrichment.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the comparative superiority claim but ties it specifically to pathway-enrichment evaluation, changing the evaluation context and thus narrowing the original, more general claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Globally sparse probabilistic PCA (GSPPCA) is shown to infer biologically relevant subsets of genes from unlabeled microarray data.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit statement about inferring biologically relevant gene subsets that does not correspond to a distinct, separately worded preprint claim (it overlaps with P3 but is presented as a separate published claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional experimental designs for spectral processing are often improperly used in the spectral-spatial processing context, leading to unfair or biased performance evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional experimental designs for spectral processing are often improperly used in the spectral-spatial processing context, leading to unfair or biased performance evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same critique of experimental design with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dependence caused by overlap between the training and testing samples may be artificially enhanced by some spatial information processing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dependence caused by overlap between the training and testing samples may be artificially enhanced by some spatial information processing methods, leading to an improper evaluation of spectral-spatial classification techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the consequence (improper evaluation) and presents the assertion with higher certainty, amplifying its impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The widely adopted pixel-based random sampling strategy is not always suitable to evaluate spectral-spatial classification algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The widely adopted pixel-based random sampling strategy is not always suitable to evaluate spectral-spatial classification algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same limitation statement about pixel-based random sampling without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel controlled random sampling strategy for spectral-spatial methods can greatly reduce the overlap between training and testing samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel controlled random sampling strategy for spectral-spatial methods can greatly reduce the overlap between training and testing samples.
alignments.rationale: The proposed controlled sampling strategy is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed sampling strategy provides more objective and accurate evaluation of spectral-spatial classification algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit evaluative conclusion about objectivity and accuracy that is not separately stated in the preprint; it summarizes the intended benefit of the proposed strategy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There exist rare but extremely dense and accessible regions of configurations in the network weight space of artificial neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There exist rare but extremely dense and accessible regions of configurations in the network weight space of artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same empirical finding about dense accessible regions in weight space with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel measure called the 'robust ensemble' (RE) suppresses trapping by isolated configurations and amplifies the role of dense regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robust ensemble (RE) measure suppresses trapping by isolated configurations and amplifies the role of dense regions in the optimization landscape.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the RE measure's intended effect with essentially the same meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived algorithms targeting robust dense states result in substantial improvements in performance for various optimization processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derived algorithms targeting robust dense states result in substantial improvements in performance for various optimization processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that algorithms derived from RE improve optimization performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The weak dependence on the number of precision bits of the weights suggests that similar reasoning applies to more conventional neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The weak dependence on the number of precision bits of the weights suggests that similar reasoning may apply to more conventional neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same tentative generalization to conventional networks; wording change is minor and preserves meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analogous algorithmic schemes can be applied to other optimization problems beyond neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analogous algorithmic schemes can be applied to other optimization problems beyond neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion that the algorithmic approach generalizes to other optimization problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed theory and algorithms based on piece-wise quadratic error potentials of subquadratic growth (PQSQ potentials) provide a flexible and computationally efficient framework for minimizing arbitrary sub-quadratic error potentials. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed theory and algorithms provide a flexible and computationally efficient framework for minimizing arbitrary sub-quadratic error potentials. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and confidence, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PQSQ-based machine learning methods achieve orders of magnitude faster computational performance than the corresponding state-of-the-art methods on synthetic and real-life datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PQSQ-based machine learning methods achieve orders of magnitude faster computational performance than the corresponding state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim (minus dataset examples) with the same high certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach improves the computational cost/accuracy trade-off in existing machine learning methods, including data approximation and regularized and sparse regression. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach leads to an improvement in the computational cost/accuracy trade-off in existing machine learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about cost/accuracy trade-offs with comparable scope and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The theory of PQSQ potentials is based on the notion of the cone of minorant functions and represents a natural approximation formalism. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This conceptual framing (cone of minorant functions) does not appear in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence process to global consensus is getting slower as the community structure becomes more prominent, and eventually might fail. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convergence process to global consensus is getting slower as the community structure becomes more prominent, and eventually might fail. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: If the inter-community connections are sufficiently dense, neither the number nor the size of the communities affects the convergence process. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results reveal the role of local communities in a global naming game in social network studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Poisson multi-Bernoulli mixture (PMBM) conjugate prior is effective for multiple extended object filtering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Poisson multi-Bernoulli mixture (PMBM) conjugate prior is effective for multiple extended object filtering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximations are necessary for tractability due to the intractably large number of terms in the PMBM density caused by unknown data associations. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating sparsity in the precision matrix allows the Gaussian variational distribution to be both flexible and parsimonious. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Efficient stochastic gradient methods are developed for the optimization of the Gaussian variational approximation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a method for structure learning in spatially distributed dynamical systems using coupled maps as synchronous update graph dynamical systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a method for structure learning in spatially distributed dynamical systems using a directed acyclic graph.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method computes globally optimal approximations of distributions, unlike established procedures that find locally maximum posterior probabilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method computes globally optimal approximations of distributions, unlike established procedures that find locally maximum posterior probabilities.
alignments.rationale: The wording and claim content are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights into when using a different number of scoring options is optimal in various settings.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows a significant reduction in computational cost and number of parameters compared to state-of-the-art deep CNNs without compromising accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: For ResNet 50, the model has 40% fewer parameters, 45% fewer floating point operations, and is 31% (12%) faster on a CPU (GPU).

alignments.pub_claim_text: For the deeper ResNet 200, the model has 48% fewer parameters and 27% fewer floating point operations while maintaining state-of-the-art accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: For GoogLeNet, the model has 7% fewer parameters and is 21% (16%) faster on a CPU (GPU).

alignments.pub_claim_text: A conceptual framework of antecedents and consequences of brands' use of textual paralanguage is presented. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates state-of-the-art results on two different datasets for the acoustic scene classification task.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Representations of probability measures in reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces allow for fully nonparametric hypothesis tests of independence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational cost of these independence testing approaches is at least quadratic in the number of observations, which can be prohibitive for large-scale datasets.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our large-scale methods demonstrate comparable performance to existing methods while using significantly less computation time and memory.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for linear classification of high-dimensional multi-way data by factorizing coefficients into weights specific to each dimension.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework allows for linear classification of high-dimensional multi-way data by factorizing coefficients into weights specific to each dimension.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coefficients for each measurement in a multi-way dataset are assumed to have low-rank structure.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We find a set of six core emotional arcs which form the essential building blocks of complex emotional trajectories.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We strengthen our findings by separately applying Matrix decomposition, supervised learning, and unsupervised learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We strengthen our findings by separately applying matrix decomposition, supervised learning, and unsupervised learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Particular emotional arcs enjoy greater success, as measured by downloads.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Particular emotional arcs enjoy greater success, as measured by downloads.
alignments.rationale: The empirical observation about success measured by downloads is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutual information between two symbols decays exponentially in any probabilistic regular grammar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutual information between two symbols decays exponentially in any probabilistic regular grammar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutual information can decay like a power law for a context-free grammar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutual information can decay like a power law for a context-free grammar. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about context-free grammars verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results about formal languages are related to the absence of phase transitions in dimensions fewer than two in classical statistical mechanics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are no phase transitions in dimensions fewer than two, which is related to the results about formal languages. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings are connected to the emergence of power-law correlations in turbulence and cosmological inflation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed joint optimization approach improves the average-case performance of TCS by employing an optimized multidimensional sensing matrix and a learned multilinear sparsifying dictionary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An extended separable approach with a closed form solution and a novel iterative non-separable method are proposed for the sensing matrix design in TCS.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multidimensional dictionary learning method that leverages the multidimensional structure is derived, considering the influence of sensing matrices in the learning process.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments using synthetic data and real images demonstrate the superiority of the proposed approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed probabilistic framework effectively models and analyzes the random behavior of energy harvesting and energy consumption in dense small cell networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Energy harvesting in small cell networks is characterized by opportunistic behavior, leading to a random variable for the amount of harvested energy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The energy consumption in the network is also a random variable due to the random arrival of users and their varying service requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The success probability for each user depends on various random factors, including energy harvesting, channel quality, and network traffic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A bandit-theoretical formulation is developed for distributed user association in scenarios where no information is available at users.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed user association scheme allows users to independently select their corresponding small base station based on a success probability metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DehazeNet is a trainable end-to-end system for medium transmission estimation that outputs a medium transmission map from a hazy image.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel nonlinear activation function called Bilateral Rectified Linear Unit (BReLU) in DehazeNet improves the quality of the recovered haze-free image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel bilateral rectified linear unit activation function in DehazeNet improves the quality of the recovered haze-free image.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that DehazeNet achieves superior performance over existing methods while remaining efficient and easy to use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that DehazeNet achieves superior performance over existing methods while remaining efficient and easy to use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new structure for machine learning classifiers improves performance at intermediate values by including physics parameters as inputs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parameterized classifier can smoothly interpolate between different learning tasks, replacing the need for multiple classifiers trained at individual values.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applications of this approach include tools parameterized by theoretical model parameters, such as particle mass, enabling improved discrimination across a range of masses.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The James–Stein (JS) estimator dominates the simple maximum-likelihood (ML) estimator when the dimension n exceeds two. (certainty=high, type=result)
